title: asterolasia correifolia var. muelleri
intro: Asterolasia correifolia var. muelleri, also known as Mueller's asterolasia, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family, commonly found in eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of limonene and other volatile compounds. Mueller's asterolasia has been reported to have various health benefits, including wound healing, antimicrobial activity, and potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and oils, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
